type: SURVEY
files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Adding backup=True and backup_suffix params to Image.save() method (src/PIL/Image.py ~line 2469-2591); will shutil.copy2 existing file to filename+suffix before opening for write. Need to know what you're touching in save().

type: SURVEY
files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Adding dry_run parameter to Image.save() method (src/PIL/Image.py ~line 2469-2591). Will process image for saving but skip actual disk write. Need to know if your feature also touches save().

type: SURVEY
files: src/PIL/Image.py
note: We both edit save(). Also note: shutil is not currently imported in Image.py (only io, os, etc) - you'll likely need 'import shutil' added near line 32-40 for backup's copy2 call. That import line is also a shared/overlapping construct we should agree on to avoid duplicate-import conflicts.

type: DRAFT
files: src/PIL/Image.py
region: Image.save() docstring
code:         """
        Saves this image under the given filename.  If no format is
        specified, the format to use is determined from the filename
        extension, if possible.

        Keyword options can be used to provide additional instructions
        to the writer. If a writer doesn't recognise an option, it is
        silently ignored. The available options are described in the
        :doc:`image format documentation
        <../handbook/image-file-formats>` for each writer.

        You can use a file object instead of a filename. In this case,
        you must always specify the format. The file object must
        implement the ``seek``, ``tell``, and ``write``
        methods, and be opened in binary mode.

        :param fp: A filename (string), os.PathLike object or file object.
        :param format: Optional format override.  If omitted, the
           format to use is determined from the filename extension.
           If a file object was used instead of a filename, this
           parameter should always be used.
        :param params: Extra parameters to the image writer. These can also be
           set on the image itself through ``encoderinfo``. This is useful when
           saving multiple images::

             # Saving XMP data to a single image
             from PIL import Image
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.save("out.mpo", xmp=b"test")

             # Saving XMP data to the second frame of an image
             from PIL import Image
             black = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1))
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.encoderinfo = {"xmp": b"test"}
             black.save("out.mpo", save_all=True, append_images=[red])

           Additional supported keyword parameters:

           * ``backup`` (bool) -- If true, and a file already exists at the
             destination, a copy of the existing file is made before it is
             overwritten. Defaults to ``False``.
           * ``backup_suffix`` (str) -- The suffix appended to the filename
             when creating a backup. Only used when ``backup`` is true.
             Defaults to ``".bak"``.
           * ``dry_run`` (bool) -- If true, the image is processed and
             encoded as normal, but the result is discarded instead of
             being written to ``fp``. Defaults to ``False``.
        :returns: None
        :exception ValueError: If the output format could not be determined
           from the file name.  Use the format option to solve this.
        :exception OSError: If the file could not be written.  The file
           may have been created, and may contain partial data.
        """
note: Merged docstring adding backup/backup_suffix and dry_run param docs. Please confirm or counter-draft.

type: DRAFT
files: src/PIL/Image.py
region: Image.save() docstring
code:         """
        Saves this image under the given filename.  If no format is
        specified, the format to use is determined from the filename
        extension, if possible.

        Keyword options can be used to provide additional instructions
        to the writer. If a writer doesn't recognise an option, it is
        silently ignored. The available options are described in the
        :doc:`image format documentation
        <../handbook/image-file-formats>` for each writer.

        You can use a file object instead of a filename. In this case,
        you must always specify the format. The file object must
        implement the ``seek``, ``tell``, and ``write``
        methods, and be opened in binary mode.

        :param fp: A filename (string), os.PathLike object or file object.
        :param format: Optional format override.  If omitted, the
           format to use is determined from the filename extension.
           If a file object was used instead of a filename, this
           parameter should always be used.
        :param params: Extra parameters to the image writer. These can also be
           set on the image itself through ``encoderinfo``. This is useful when
           saving multiple images::

             # Saving XMP data to a single image
             from PIL import Image
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.save("out.mpo", xmp=b"test")

             # Saving XMP data to the second frame of an image
             from PIL import Image
             black = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1))
             red = Image.new("RGB", (1, 1), "#f00")
             red.encoderinfo = {"xmp": b"test"}
             black.save("out.mpo", save_all=True, append_images=[red])

           Additionally, the following keyword-only options are supported:

           * ``dry_run`` (bool): If True, the image is fully processed
             for saving (including format conversion and compression) but
             the result is discarded instead of being written to fp or
             disk. Any errors that would normally occur during saving are
             still raised, making this useful for validating that an image
             can be saved without the cost of disk I/O. Defaults to
             ``False``.
           * ``backup`` (bool): If ``True`` and the target file already
             exists, a copy of the existing file is made before it is
             overwritten. Defaults to ``False``.
           * ``backup_suffix`` (str): The suffix appended to the filename
             for the backup copy when ``backup`` is ``True``. Defaults to
             ``".bak"``.
        :returns: None
        :exception ValueError: If the output format could not be determined
           from the file name.  Use the format option to solve this.
        :exception OSError: If the file could not be written.  The file
           may have been created, and may contain partial data.
        """

note: Docstring for save(), documenting dry_run, backup, and backup_suffix as extra keyword params in the existing :param params: section. Please confirm or counter-draft.
